name: USP30 inhibition induced tissue-specific mitophagy in reporter mice
summary: ST-539 supports pharmacological tractability of targeted mitophagy but remains preclinical and mechanistic.
statement: Pharmacological USP30 inhibition with ST-539 modulated PINK1/Parkin-dependent mitophagy and induced tissue-specific mitophagy in mouse reporter models, including efficient cardiac mitophagy in the reported dosing context.
quantitative_note: The study used direct mitophagy reporter measurements rather than inferred mitochondrial-health proxies.
caveats: This is preclinical mechanistic evidence, not an aging efficacy study.
The finding supports mitophagy-marker activation rather than durable functional benefit.
USP30 modulation may need tissue-specific dose thresholds and safety assessment before aging-relevant interpretation.
